tier: A2-3.1 production mutation route on the certified COW engine (root file write/delete/rename)
host: Cherry Servers bare-metal Intel (qemu-system-x86_64 -enable-kvm, macOS Sequoia recovery, apfs_kext 2332.101.1)
summary: The File Management bridge (FileManagementFileSystemBridge) now routes APFS root-file write/delete/rename onto the Apple-certified crash-safe in-place COW commit engine (commitRawFileWrite/Delete/Rename) instead of the legacy single-chunk raw writers. The file-write primitive is create-or-replace: a new name is one insert commit; an existing name is a delete-then-insert replace, both on the certified COW engine.
unit_tests: 125/125 partition-core tests pass, including apfsWriter_inPlaceFileWriteCreatesThenReplaces (create -> readable v1 at xid 3; replace -> readable v2 at xid 5, one entry, new size)
linux_apfsck: apfsck -w clean on the create container (fw1, xid 2->3) and the replace container (fw2, xid 2->3 create then 3->4 delete + 4->5 insert) - only the pre-existing benign 'Data stream xfield: wrong flags.' apfsck-vs-Apple divergence

create.kernel_read: the macOS kernel auto-mounted fw1 read-write and read /Volumes/FWTEST/doc.txt = 'version-one-content' (20 bytes), content + size matching

replace.operation: commit-image-file-write doc.txt overwritten with 'version-two-which-is-noticeably-longer' (39 bytes): delete xid 3 -> 4 then insert xid 4 -> 5
replace.kernel_continuation: writer emitted xid 5; the macOS kernel auto-mounted fw2 read-write and CONTINUED the checkpoint to transaction ID 7 (two kernel commits past the writer)
replace.command: fsck_apfs -n /dev/disk4 (unmounted after the kernel auto-mounted it read-write)
replace.container_verdict: The container /dev/disk4 appears to be OK.
replace.volume_verdict: The volume /dev/rdisk4s1 (UUID 16A89DCD-487D-49E9-991B-29579FE44C72) appears to be OK.
replace.volume_provenance: The volume FWTEST was formatted by S.A.K. Utility APFS writer and last modified by apfs_kext (2332.101.1).

scope_note: This increment routes only ROOT FILE write/delete/rename onto the COW engine. Directory create/delete, directory-child file write/delete, patch, and change-label remain on the legacy raw writer pending COW parity (follow-on increments). The production mutation cap is raised by the switch (the COW engine container cap is 32 TiB vs the old single-chunk writer).
